title: ALTER TABLE
content:
### ALTER TABLE Statement

ALTER TABLE modifies the structure of an existing table. You can add, drop, or modify columns and constraints.

---

### ADD Column

```sql
ALTER TABLE employees
ADD COLUMN phone VARCHAR(20);

-- MySQL
ALTER TABLE employees ADD phone VARCHAR(20);

-- Add with default value
ALTER TABLE employees
ADD COLUMN department VARCHAR(100) DEFAULT 'General';
```

### DROP Column

```sql
ALTER TABLE employees
DROP COLUMN phone;

-- PostgreSQL
ALTER TABLE employees DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS phone;
```

### MODIFY Column

```sql
-- MySQL
ALTER TABLE employees
MODIFY COLUMN salary DECIMAL(12,2) NOT NULL;

-- PostgreSQL
ALTER TABLE employees
ALTER COLUMN salary TYPE DECIMAL(12,2);

-- SQL Server
ALTER TABLE employees
ALTER COLUMN salary DECIMAL(12,2) NOT NULL;
```

### RENAME Column

```sql
-- PostgreSQL
ALTER TABLE employees RENAME COLUMN phone TO mobile;

-- MySQL
ALTER TABLE employees RENAME COLUMN phone TO mobile;

-- SQL Server
EXEC sp_rename 'employees.phone', 'mobile', 'COLUMN';
```

### RENAME Table

```sql
ALTER TABLE employees RENAME TO staff;
-- Or:
RENAME TABLE employees TO staff;
```

### Add/Drop Constraints

```sql
ALTER TABLE employees
ADD CONSTRAINT pk_emp PRIMARY KEY (id);

ALTER TABLE employees
ADD CONSTRAINT fk_dept FOREIGN KEY (dept_id) REFERENCES departments(id);

ALTER TABLE employees DROP CONSTRAINT pk_emp;
```

> **Key Point:** ALTER TABLE operations can be expensive on large tables. Some operations (like dropping a column) may require a full table rebuild in certain databases.
codeExample:
ALTER TABLE employees
ADD COLUMN middle_name VARCHAR(50),
ADD COLUMN date_of_birth DATE,
MODIFY COLUMN salary DECIMAL(12,2) NOT NULL;

quiz.question: Which statement modifies the structure of an existing table?
Can ALTER TABLE rename a column?
